attacking_style: Hybrid sprinter-opportunist who contests intermediate sprints aggressively and launches breakaway attacks from the peloton mid-stage, not limiting himself to traditional sprint finales. Shows willingness to sprint early (300m+) with track-derived power.
team_tactics: Bahrain deploys him in dual role as points classification contender and leadout man, creating execution vulnerabilities. Sprint train struggles under high-pressure scenarios. Benefits from crash-reduced fields rather than organized leadout trains.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where track power background provides advantage through centerline positioning and sustained high wattage. Excels when field is reduced by crashes or splits, avoiding pure mass sprints.
vulnerability: Dual-role demands compromise both sprint outcomes and leadout effectiveness. Physical resilience questioned (required mid-race medical treatment). Early sprint launches leave him exposed if mistimed. Team's sprint train execution unreliable under pressure.
key_patterns: Actively hunts intermediate sprint points for classification, not just stage wins (3 observations).
Launches sprint earlier than optimal, showing track racing instincts over road positioning (2 observations).
Dual leadout/sprinter role creates finale execution failures under pressure (2 observations).
Breaks from peloton mid-race showing opportunistic attacking beyond pure sprint profile (2 observations).
Benefits from reduced bunch sprints; debut win came after crash split field (2 observations).
form_trajectory: neutral: Won points jersey at 2023 Giro but final sprint execution deteriorated. Medical issues mid-race and failed dual-role attempts suggest physical/tactical limits exposed over three weeks despite classification success.
